title: Generalized convex toric domains and symplectic embedding problems
speaker: Nicki Magill
abstract: A convex toric domain  is a 4-dimensional subset of , defined as the preimage of a bounded convex region  in the positive quadrant of  under the moment map. We consider how geometric features of  such as the curviness of its boundary and its affine perimeter impact symplectic packing problems. Some of our results come from considering the asymptotics of the ECH capacities. These capacities are known to obey a Weyl law and thus detect the volume of . We show that their subleading asymptotics detect the affine perimeter of . We'll discuss how these asymptotic results lead to new applications in symplectic embedding problems. This is based on joint work with Dan Cristofaro-Gardiner and Dusa McDuff.

proof_stages.label: Symplectic embeddings and generalized convex toric domains
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up four-dimensional symplectic embeddings, recalls volume and nonsqueezing obstructions, and defines generalized convex toric domains as moment-map preimages of compact convex regions in the positive quadrant.

proof_stages.label: Affine perimeter as the toric boundary invariant
proof_stages.summary: An SL(2,Z)-invariant affine length and affine perimeter are defined so that rational linear boundary pieces contribute lattice length while irrational or curved pieces contribute zero.

proof_stages.label: ECH capacities and subleading asymptotics
proof_stages.summary: ECH capacities are introduced through monotonicity, scaling, Weyl-law, and disjoint-union properties, the subleading terms e_k are defined by subtracting the Weyl leading term, and the main theorem is stated that liminf e_k equals negative one-half the affine perimeter.

proof_stages.label: Weight expansion capacity formula
proof_stages.summary: A generalized weight sequence is constructed by enclosing the moment region in a minimal triangle and iteratively decomposing complementary concave regions, yielding a formula for ECH capacities of X_omega in terms of capacities of balls.

proof_stages.label: Derivation of the perimeter liminf theorem
proof_stages.summary: The perimeter and volume are expressed in the weight sequence as 3b minus the sum of weights and b^2 minus the sum of squared weights, and ball capacity estimates plus an optimization argument identify the liminf of the subleading terms.

proof_stages.label: Full-filling perimeter obstruction and packing stability
proof_stages.summary: Using ECH monotonicity, scaling, and the subleading theorem, full fillings of equal-volume domains are shown to force a reversed affine-perimeter inequality, which extends to disjoint unions and rules out finite zero-perimeter domains fully filling a ball or CP2.

proof_stages.label: Ellipsoid embedding functions and accumulation-point obstruction
proof_stages.summary: The ellipsoid embedding function and infinite staircases are defined, the generalized accumulation-point theorem is stated, and the perimeter inequality is applied to show that an irrational ellipsoid E(1,a) cannot fully fill X_omega when a is below the predicted accumulation point.

proof_stages.label: Smooth-boundary staircase classification and method comparison
proof_stages.summary: A further theorem classifies smooth-boundary convex toric domains with infinite staircases as only scalings of the ball, E(1,2), and E(1,3/2), after which the speaker contrasts ECH-capacity and exceptional-class obstructions and lists open questions.
